title: Zhuangzi, Chapter 27: Imputed Words
description: Chapter 27 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.
introduction: Chapter 27 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.

translation.text: Nine out of ten of my sayings are imputed; seven out of ten are weighty sayings. Imputed words make up nine-tenths because they borrow outside support to discuss matters.
